properties.description: Provides immunizations for infants, children, youth, and adults. Immunization services are offered at public health units, various community locations, schools, and through community health professionals such a doctors and pharmacists. See website for more information including adult immunizations, influenza vaccines, COVID-19 immunization clinics, vaccine providers, travel immunization, and infant, child and youth immunization.

Provides health information for infants, children and youth on topics such as immunization, dental health, hearing, speech and language, school health, nutrition, parenting education, and mental health. Parents and guardians can also learn about their child's growth and development on topics such as physical development, speech and language, sleep, injury prevention, and social emotional development.

The HPV vaccine is safe and effective at preventing cancers caused by the Human Papillomavirus (HPV). The HPV vaccines are given as a series of either 2 or 3 doses over a 6 month period and can be given at many pharmacies throughout B.C. Supply of the HPV vaccine is guaranteed to be available at this location.

Provides support for pregnant women and new moms, their babies, children and families. Created by women, for women, the program is offered by public health nurses who will work in partnership with patients. Nurses are available by phone or in-person at Island Health public health units across the region. Services include prenatal information, postpartum physical and emotional support, breastfeeding support, family and community health referrals, child health clinics and immunizations starting at two months of age, and access to Baby's Best Chance handbook.
